TextENGNew: The donated items slated for the bargain auction had been gathered in the meeting hall at Furude Shrine.
changeReason: New translation specifies "in the meeting hall at Furude Shrine" rather than just "at the Furude shrine", adding the specific location (集会所) from the Japanese.

TextENGNew: ``Whoa, there's all kinds of stuff here...!@k@vS12/01/180100257. I expected gift-set towels and worn-out furniture, but there's also electronics and daily goods!``
changeReason: New translation specifies "electronics and daily goods" where old vaguely says "way more things than that", adding concrete detail from the Japanese.

TextENGNew: ``Wow -- they've even got TVs and refrigerators.@k@vS12/02/180200063. And these are practically brand new!``

``Hahaha, surprised?@k@vS12/03/180300107. We got donations from businesses connected to the Sonozaki family -- floor models, leftover stock, things that can't be sold as new but are basically unused!``
changeReason: Old says "stores my family owns" (first-person); new says "businesses connected to the Sonozaki family" (third-person framing), changing the speaker perspective.

TextENGNew: ``If you don't mind them being a model behind, they're pretty impressive, right?``
changeReason: Old was missing opening quotation mark, indicating broken formatting from a split line.

TextENGNew: ``The sun-bleached 'Brand New!' stickers on these appliances have a certain melancholy to them.``
``No, no, Satoko -- that's actually a selling point.@k@vS12/01/180100259. A slightly dusty but unused item beats somebody's hand-me-down any day.``
``...Mii. Youngsters these days sure are fastidious.``
changeReason: Rika's catchphrase changed from "Meep" to "Mii" (closer to the Japanese "みー").

TextENGNew: ``Ahahaha, I don't mind either way. As long as it's cheap, this kind of thing is perfectly fine.``
``Rena-san, you seem pretty interested in the furniture.@k@vS12/06/180600032. Are you thinking of living on your own?``
``Oh, no.@k@vS12/02/180200066. My dad and I have been talking about redecorating the house.@k@vS12/02/180200067. So I was wondering if there was anything we could use.``
changeReason: Old mentions "checking out recycled ones"; new generalizes to "wondering if there was anything we could use", omitting the recycled goods detail.

TextENGNew: ``You're changing the furniture too?@k@vS12/04/180400098. That's quite a big undertaking.``
``Yep.@k@vS12/02/180200069. My dad finally got a new job recently.``
``So we want to make it a fresh start -- go big with the whole makeover.``
``...Your father... got a new job?``
Rika-chan asked casually, but I was thinking the same thing.
...Did that mean Rena's father had been unemployed until recently...?

``Yeah.@k@vS12/02/180200072. For a whole year after he and Mom split up, he didn't even try to find work.@k@vS12/02/180200073. Rena was mad about it too.@k@vS12/02/180200074. I kept telling him, 'Get a proper job already~!'``
Rena said it with her usual smile, as though it were the most natural thing in the world, so it took me a moment to register just how heavy what she'd said actually was.
...If what Rena was saying was literal, it meant her parents had divorced and her home situation was far from ideal.
But Rena spoke about it so normally that the mood never turned heavy.
``Your mother and father... separated?``
``Yep.@k@vS12/02/180200076. They divorced back in the town where we used to live.``
``So Dad and I came back to Hinamizawa.@k@vS12/02/180200078. But he just couldn't recover from the shock of the divorce, and he was a mess for a long time...``
``Divorce and heartbreak -- the deeper the love, the deeper the wound.@k@vS12/06/180600034. But I don't think the depth of that pain is necessarily a bad thing.``
``Ahahaha, if only it were that romantic.@k@vS12/02/180200081. Dad ended up frequenting some shady establishment and getting close to some woman there.``
changeReason: Old vaguely says "spending money at the bar"; new adds "getting close to some woman there", introducing the relationship detail from the Japanese.

TextENGNew: ``Rena's mom was apparently a pretty well-known designer.@k@vS12/03/180300110. She was making quite a lot of money.``
changeReason: Old says Rena's mom "is" a designer (present tense); new says "was apparently" (past tense with hedging), changing the temporal framing.

TextENGNew: ``So her father got a hefty settlement.``
``That's why Dad could get by without working.@k@vS12/02/180200084. And that's what slowed down his recovery.``

``That makes sense.@k@vS12/01/180100261. If he'd been strapped for cash, he would've had no choice but to work.@k@vS12/01/180100262. Not having to struggle can actually make it harder to get back on your feet.``
changeReason: Old ends with advice about keeping busy; new says "Not having to struggle can actually make it harder to get back on your feet" -- opposite framing of the causal relationship.

TextENGNew: ``The ordinary routine of waking up, going to school or work, coming home in the evening, eating dinner, and going to bed --@k@vS12/06/180600036. once that pattern breaks down, it's surprisingly hard to rebuild.``
changeReason: Old abbreviates to a short summary; new fully translates the detailed daily routine description from the Japanese.

TextENGNew: ``I went through something similar after I left the academy -- I spent a while living without purpose, so I understand.``
``...Shutting yourself in at home and wallowing alone is the worst thing you can do.``
``That's right -- exactly what Rika-chan said.``
``So I had a frank talk with Dad.@k@vS12/02/180200087. About his responsibilities as head of the household, about standing on his own as a parent -- that sort of thing.``
``Heh, Rena usually seems carefree, but when it really counts, she's got it together.@k@vS12/01/180100264. And she doesn't pull her punches, even with her own parents.``
``Ahaha, it wasn't that impressive.@k@vS12/02/180200089. I was pretty shocked at first too.``
``...Because I found out Dad was lavishing money on some woman I didn't know...@k@vS12/02/180200091. As his daughter, that was a real blow.``
``...And then what did you do, Rena...?``
``Well...@k@vS12/02/180200093. Stewing over it alone got me absolutely nowhere.@k@vS12/02/180200094. The more I thought about it by myself, the worse it got...``
``So I opened up to Mii-chan and asked for her advice.``
``All I did was listen and nod along.@k@vS12/03/180300113. Rena was the one who organized her own thoughts, decided she needed to talk to her father properly, and then went and did it.``
``No, that's important.@k@vS12/01/180100266. Talking to someone else really does help you think about your problems more calmly.``
``...Hmm.@k@vS12/06/180600039. Is that how it works...?@k@vS12/06/180600040. If something can be solved just by talking about it, then it's not much of a problem, is it?``
``That's not true, Shii-chan.``
``Before I talked to Mii-chan, the only things going through my head were dark ideas -- like ambushing that woman.@k@vS12/02/180200097. I couldn't think of anything constructive at all.``
changeReason: Old says "how to hurt that lady"; new says "ambushing that woman" (闇討ち), which is more specific and violent.

TextENGNew: ``You have to start with talking it out.``
changeReason: Old says "Talking it out with him was the important thing" (specific to the father); new says "You have to start with talking it out" (general principle).

TextENGNew: ``...And what happened after you talked it out with him...?``
``Well...@k@vS12/02/180200101. We figured out that Dad's aimlessness was all because of the money.@k@vS12/02/180200102. So I took control of the household finances.``
``Now Dad gets a monthly allowance from me.``
``Oh ho ho ho!@k@vS12/04/180400101. It sounds like the parent and child have switched roles in your house!``
When Satoko laughed, everyone else joined in.
I hesitated for just a moment, unsure whether it was okay to laugh, but seeing Rena laughing too, I joined in.
``In the end, having too much money with no obligations was what led Rena's father astray.``
``Once he regained a sense of money's value, he stopped going to that place and started feeling the need to work properly.``
``But the timing was really good.@k@vS12/02/180200105. Right when he was about to put a down payment on an apartment for that woman, I stepped in.``
``You talk about it with a smile, but when you actually listen to the details... it's a pretty serious story.@k@vS12/01/180100268. One wrong step and the whole family could have fallen apart...``
``That's true.@k@vS12/02/180200108. If I hadn't talked to Mii-chan back then -- if I'd just kept brooding until today -- I think by now...``
``...Yeah.@k@vS12/02/180200110. I might have been thinking about killing that woman.``
Everyone laughed, saying what a dangerous thing that was to say.
Rena laughed too.
But deep in her eyes, there was a trace of something serious.
...She probably wasn't joking at all. Back then, she had genuinely considered murder.
If your own father got ensnared by some strange woman who started draining the family's savings...
changeReason: Old says "She seduced Rena's father" (third-person statement of fact); new says "If your own father got ensnared" (second-person hypothetical) -- completely different narrative framing.

TextENGNew: ...Knowing my own short temper, I'd probably think the same thing.
Thinking about it that way, Rena had done the right thing -- instead of doing something rash, she'd confided in a friend. That took real courage.
``That was really admirable.@k@vS12/01/180100270. Family problems aren't easy to talk about -- they're embarrassing to share.``
``Ahahaha, you're right.@k@vS12/02/180200112. It took me a while to work up the courage to tell Mii-chan. But a dream I had one night finally pushed me over the edge.``
``...A dream?``
``Yeah.@k@vS12/02/180200115. ...Back then, I'd lie in bed every night agonizing over it alone.@k@vS12/02/180200116. So I had bad dreams a lot.``
In my dream, I imagined what would happen if I just kept doing nothing -- kept wallowing in my misery.
...I didn't know if it was mere imagination, or a prophetic dream showing me the future months or years down the road.
``In the future my dream showed me, Dad had gotten even closer to that woman, and eventually he started bringing her into our home.``
Neither Dad nor I smoked, yet somehow an ashtray had appeared in the living room.
The futon in the guest room had become the permanent territory of someone who wasn't family.
In the bathroom, there was a toothbrush I would never choose, and a shampoo I would never buy.
...Such a deeply unwelcome sight.
``That sounds... truly awful... no, truly painful...``
...I'd never heard it from Satoko herself, but I'd been told she lost her parents too.
She'd been placed in the care of an uncle and aunt she didn't get along with, and had gone through a great deal of hardship.@k ...I was pretty sure someone had told me that.
With that in mind, it seemed like Satoko understood Rena's painful situation very well.
``...Yes.@k@vS12/02/180200119. It was a truly painful situation.``
``Gradually, I lost my place at home -- I was being cornered little by little.@k@vS12/02/180200121. And then, over some trivial trigger, I... snapped.``
changeReason: Old says "Eventually, I felt like I didn't belong"; new says "I was being cornered" and adds "And then... I snapped" -- more explicit about the violent breaking point.

TextENGNew: ``That's an awfully vivid dream...@k@vS12/06/180600042. What happened next?``
``And then I was a murderer.@k@vS12/02/180200123. ...I was terrified that someone would find out, cowering and afraid.``
changeReason: Old includes "I spent all day at the place I buried her"; new omits the burial vigil detail.

TextENGNew: ``I felt like if I left that spot, someone would discover the body, so I just stayed there... forever and ever.``
``...What a terrible future.@k@vS12/01/180100272. I'm glad it was only a dream.``
``I wonder... was it really just a dream?``
``...I think... that was the future that would have come true if I'd just done nothing.``
``So when I woke up the next morning, I was so scared I couldn't stop shaking.@k@vS12/02/180200129. I even imagined I could smell perfume in our house that didn't belong to me or Dad...``
``And I kept getting more and more afraid, wondering whether that dream would come true next year, next month... or maybe even tomorrow.``
``...Once I'd been pushed that far, I had no hesitation about opening up to Mii-chan.``

``I remember Rena coming to me with this incredibly solemn expression, saying she had a secret she needed to discuss.@k@vS12/03/180300118. I'll be honest -- I thought she was going to tell me about a pregnancy scare or something.``
``Well, sure, we can laugh about it now. But at the time, it really wasn't a laughing matter.``
``Mii-chan was so dependable.@k@vS12/02/180200133. She listened to everything I had to say calmly, and helped me find a realistic way forward.``
changeReason: Old includes "we decided that I needed to talk with him face-to-face"; new omits this concluding resolution.

TextENGNew: ``...Perhaps... it wasn't just a dream.``
``Ahaha, you're right.@k@vS12/02/180200136. Maybe it was more than just a dream.``
``Without that dream, you might never have opened up to Mion.@k@vS12/01/180100274. It may have been the most important dream of your life.``
``...A dream, huh.``
``You've had dreams too, Shion?@k@vS12/03/180300121. Really?``
Shion said something under her breath.
Only Mion caught what she said.
I couldn't hear the words, but whatever they were, the teasing vanished from Mion's face -- so it must have been something serious.
...Incidentally, as a side note.
Shion normally attended school in Okinomiya.@k And yet, she'd been showing up at the Hinamizawa school quite often lately.
I had no idea what made her bother trekking all the way out to this remote mountain school,@k ...but she seemed to enjoy fussing over Satoko.
What connection Shion and Satoko shared was a complete mystery to me.
...Wait -- wasn't Shion not particularly close to Satoko before...?
...Huh?@k Did I imagine that?
Hadn't Shion and Satoko always been on good terms?@k ...???
``That's why I learned something important.@k@vS12/02/180200138. Talking to someone is so, so important.``
This time, we'll play normally. We'll laugh together normally. We'll fall in love normally.@k We'll never doubt each other.@k We'll always believe in each other.
changeReason: Old ends with "I want everyone to think that they can talk to me"; new changes to "Because having someone who'll listen is what saved me" -- shifts from request to personal testimony.

TextENGNew: ``Friends aren't just playmates.@k@vS12/02/180200140. They're the people you can open up to about what truly matters... ahaha, saying it like this makes it sound so obvious!``
``You're right.@k@vS12/01/180100276. ...If that's the case, then you didn't just solve a family problem -- you also learned how important your friends really are.``

``Yeah.@k@vS12/02/180200142. So if anyone is ever struggling, I want to be there for them. I want to listen and I want to help.@k@vS12/02/180200143. Because having someone who'll listen is what saved me.``
``...Mii.@k@vS12/05/180500226. Rena is saying something very, very important.``
changeReason: Rika's catchphrase changed from "Meep" to "Mii" (closer to the Japanese "みー").

TextENGNew: Rika-chan was right.
...So none of us joked or teased. We just nodded in silence.
Just then, we heard the sound of the meeting hall entrance being locked with a loud rattle, and everyone jumped.
'Locked' wasn't quite right, though.
Someone coming from outside, not realizing the door was already unlocked, had tried to unlock it -- and ended up locking it instead.
So when they tried to open the door -- which they thought was now unlocked -- it wouldn't budge, and they rattled it loudly.
``Oh my?@k@vS12/00/womA18002. Someone's already inside?``
An older woman's voice, the presence of several people, and the sound of knocking.
``Oh -- sorry!@k@vS12/03/180300123. I'll open it right up!``
Mion called out loudly enough for them to hear and went to unlock the door.
From the entrance, I could hear Mion exchanging greetings with some ladies from the neighborhood association.
``Hey everyone, can you lend me a hand?``
``What's up? What happened?``
The reason Mion called us over was immediately apparent.
The committee members had brought another donation for the auction.@k It was a rather old china cabinet.
We all pitched in to carry it inside.
Stepping out through the entrance to help, I noticed the sky was already turning crimson, and at some point the chorus of cicadas had shifted from the daytime kind to the evening higurashi.
changeReason: Old omits the sky turning crimson (朱色); new adds "the sky was already turning crimson", restoring a visual detail from the Japanese.

TextENGNew: ``Oh, Maebara-kun, I heard you agreed to host the auction!@k@vS12/00/womA18004. Everyone's been saying there's no one better for the job!``
changeReason: Old says "everyone thinks you're the best man for that job"; new drops the final line "We're all looking forward to seeing your showmanship!"

TextENGNew: ``Ah, ahaha...!@k@vS12/01/180100279. I'll do my best, so, uh, please don't set your expectations too high.``
``Don't listen to him!``
changeReason: Both old and new are interpretive renderings of the playful exclamation そんなこと言っちゃって; new's 'Don't listen to him!' is slightly more natural but the difference is modest.

TextENGNew: ``He came here today specifically to look over the merchandise and start working on his sales pitches.@k@vS12/03/180300127. You can look forward to a great show on the day!``
changeReason: Old implies the lady continues speaking; new clarifies it's Mion speaking about Keiichi in third person ("He came here today").

TextENGNew: ``Well then, I'll have to make sure I'm there to see it!``
changeReason: Old says the speaker will do their best; new says they'll make sure to come watch -- completely different meaning (performing vs attending).

TextENGNew: The other ladies were all smiles, saying they were looking forward to it.
...I hadn't been living here all that long since I moved.
And yet, they'd recommended me for an important role at the festival.
That meant they'd accepted me -- recognized me as one of their own, even in this short time.
I was someone who'd once caused such disgrace in my old neighborhood that I couldn't walk the streets in daylight.@k ...For someone like me to be welcomed by a community like this.
I was genuinely happy.
So not just for my friends, but for these villagers I was still learning the faces and names of --@k I was determined to put on a show they'd enjoy, for recommending me and believing in me.
``What are you going to do, Mion-chan?@k@vS12/00/womA18008. Are you staying?``
``No, we're heading home now too.@k@vS12/03/180300129. Right, Kei-chan?``
``Yeah.@k@vS12/01/180100281. Now that I've got a feel for the goods, the rest is improvisation.@k@vS12/01/180100282. I'll just wing it on the day!``
``I'm not really interested in Watanagashi itself, but I do want to see Kei-chan's performance.@k@vS12/06/180600045. Maybe I'll come.``
``You absolutely must come, Shion-san!@k@vS12/04/180400104. It's so much more fun when everyone's together!``
``I object!!@k@vS12/03/180300131. Shion lives in Okinomiya -- she should go to the Okinomiya festival!``
``Ahahaha, I vote for Shii-chan coming.@k@vS12/02/180200146. Mii-chan gets so much more entertaining when Shii-chan's around!``
``...By majority vote, Shii's participation is hereby confirmed.``
``There you have it, Sis.``
``You might end up in my shadow on the day, but let's get along, shall we?``
``Uuuugh!!``
``I hate you Shion, I hate you Shion, I hate you Shioooon!!``
Whatever Mion says, they really are close sisters.
The more Mion got worked up, the more obvious their closeness became -- by at least thirty percent.
``All right, let's head out.``
``Does everyone have everything?@k@vS12/02/180200149. I'm turning off the lights.``
We checked for anything left behind and made sure there was nothing that could start a fire, then all headed outside.
I looked around the shrine grounds while Mion locked up the meeting hall.
These grounds, which felt almost too spacious for Hinamizawa, would soon be transformed into the site of the village's biggest celebration of the year.
``This whole big space is going to be one massive festival, huh?@k@vS12/01/180100284. I really can't picture it at all.@k@vS12/01/180100285. I can't wait.``
``There'll be stalls everywhere and huge crowds.@k@vS12/02/180200151. I bet it'll blow your mind, Keiichi-kun!``
``I wish they'd stop being stingy about doing it once a year and hold something every season instead.``
``Come to think of it, there is someone who visits every season.``
``Huh?@k@vS12/06/180600049. Who are you talking about?``
``Oh, I know who you mean.@k@vS12/01/180100287. That photographer-looking guy I met the other day, right?@k@vS12/01/180100288. What was his name...``
``...You've met Tomitake, Keiichi?``
``Right, right -- Tomitake-san!@k@vS12/01/180100290. I ran into him on my way home the other day.@k@vS12/01/180100291. Mion and Rena told me about him, that's all.``
``He's got this surprisingly buff build, but he's really modest and unassuming.@k@vS12/01/180100293. He comes to Hinamizawa every season -- an enthusiastic travel photographer, supposedly.``
``Self-proclaimed, I should note.@k@vS12/04/180400107. Oh ho ho ho!``
``When he shows up, it always makes me feel like Watanagashi is right around the corner.@k@vS12/03/180300136. And once Watanagashi's over, summer really starts in earnest.``
``That's true.@k@vS12/02/180200153. But this year the heat came early -- the cicadas are already singing.``
``They're saying on TV that the weather is unusual this year.@k@vS12/04/180400109. Something about global warming or whatever.``
``Heh, a scorching summer? Bring it on!@k@vS12/01/180100295. A cool summer just doesn't feel right.``
``I agree.@k@vS12/06/180600051. The weather matching the season just puts you in a better mood.@k@vS12/06/180600052. It'll be a great summer.``
changeReason: Old adds "It'll be a great summer" not present in Japanese; new more closely renders the Japanese about weather matching seasons.

TextENGNew: ``Let's be as fired up as the summer heat this year!@k@vS12/03/180300138. For Watanagashi -- let me see, how many are we?@k@vS12/03/180300139. The six of us, it's decided!``
changeReason: Old includes "battle of The Six Evils" (六凶爆闘); new omits this dramatic group title and simplifies.

TextENGNew: ``...This year's Watanagashi will be wonderful too, with all of us together.``
``Ahahaha, yeah.@k@vS12/02/180200155. I hope we have lots of fun.``
``Hey Rena, don't you know?@k@vS12/01/180100297. When Rika-chan says something, it's not just wishful thinking.@k@vS12/01/180100298. She's basically prophesying.``
``So when Rika-chan says the festival will be fun, it absolutely will be.@k@vS12/01/180100300. Because this is fate!@k@vS12/01/180100301. Ha ha ha!``
Rika-chan smiled her signature 'nipah~.'
It was a truly heartfelt smile -- one that made everyone believe that the day of Watanagashi would be wonderful.
``The auction won't start right away, will it?``
``Nah.@k@vS12/03/180300141. It's scheduled right before the ritual dance, so if we start hanging out in the afternoon, there'll be plenty of time to play together first.``
``That's good.``
``It's your first festival here, after all. It'd be a shame if you couldn't enjoy it with us.``
``After Kei-chan's auction, we get to watch Rika-chama's ritual dance?@k@vS12/06/180600054. Not bad at all.``
``...That means I won't get to watch Keiichi's performance to the end... I'll have to leave partway to prepare for the dance.``
changeReason: Old says "shrine maiden outfit"; new uses "miko outfit" -- different localization choice for the Japanese term.

TextENGNew: ``Oh, Rika-chan, you're performing too?@k@vS12/01/180100304. What's a ritual dance?@k@vS12/01/180100305. I mean, is it one of those shrine maiden dances?``
``As the name Watanagashi suggests, there's a dance and ceremony to sanctify the cotton from futons.@k@vS12/02/180200157. Rika-chan works so hard at it -- you have to watch, Keiichi-kun!``
changeReason: Old says "paying respect to the cotton"; new says "sanctify the cotton from futons" -- different ritual characterization (respect vs sanctification).

TextENGNew: ``And, and, and!@k@vS12/02/180200159. When Rika-chan dances in her miko outfit, wobbling under the weight of the ritual implement, it's soooo adorable!``
``I'm taking her home with meee!!``
Come to think of it, I'd never seen a real shrine maiden before!
If it earned Rena's 'take her home' declaration, it had to be extraordinarily cute.
I was getting more and more excited about Watanagashi.
Me, Rena, Mion, Shion, Satoko, and Rika-chan -- six of us.
We'd definitely have the time of our lives on the night of Watanagashi.
How many days left?
In any case, I'd never looked forward to a festival this much in my entire life...
